scenario: Vanta Insurance models claim handling in Camunda 8.8 across 9000 claims per day. A modeller wants to set a timer catch event to either a hard-coded value of PT2H or to a value computed from claim data, and asks how to tell the engine whether the attribute is an expression or a static value so the deployed process behaves as expected.
question: How does the engine distinguish an expression from a static value for a BPMN attribute such as a timer definition?

options.text: The engine treats every value as a FEEL expression by default; the modeller must wrap any intended static value in double quotes such as "PT2H" for the engine to skip evaluation and use the literal at activation time
An expression must start with an equals sign such as = remainingTime; without the leading equals sign the value is used as a static value such as PT2H, and a static string is not enclosed in quotes

Expressions are marked by a leading dollar sign such as $remainingTime and statics are marked by a leading hash such as #PT2H; without either prefix the engine raises a deployment-time validation error on the attribute

Expression versus static value is selected by a separate boolean attribute on the BPMN element; when that attribute is true the engine parses the value as FEEL, otherwise the engine reads the value as a literal at activation time

a.text: Incorrect. The documented rule is the opposite: values are static by default and become an expression only when they begin with an equals sign; the docs also state that a static string must not be enclosed in quotes.

b.text: Correct. The expressions concept page states that expressions always start with an equals sign, that values without the prefix are used as static values such as a job type string or a job retries number, and that a static string must not be enclosed in quotes.

c.text: Incorrect. There are no dollar-sign or hash prefixes documented for FEEL expressions or static values on BPMN attributes; the only documented marker is the leading equals sign for an expression.

d.text: Incorrect. There is no separate boolean attribute that toggles expression versus static interpretation; the leading equals sign on the value itself selects expression mode according to the concept page.

explanation: Expressions on BPMN attributes always start with an equals sign such as = remainingTime, and the text after the equals sign is the actual expression. If the value does not start with the prefix it is treated as a static value such as PT2H, and a static string must not be enclosed in quotes.
